code_checks.evidence: Shared lifecycle zip construction remains centralized.
Productized reports can use one readiness contract instead of bespoke gates.
Delivery objects can share operational lifecycle summaries.
Delivery readiness projection can be reused by support and productized report flows.
Support closure state is represented as a typed projection.
Support packet artifact listing uses one manifest iterator.
Support artifact download paths route through one path-safe resolver.
Support zip construction uses shared profile metadata per packet kind.
Support zip verification audits write through one metadata-only storage helper.
Productized commercial reports reuse delivery readiness rather than local readiness copies.
Productized commercial reports expose the same operational lifecycle projection.
Harness guide declares the delivery maturity audit as a fast freshness check.

limitations: The audit is static and local-first; it does not run live GW2 API calls or provider credentials.
Release closure still requires python harness/run_stage_gate.py release before a milestone handoff.
Future domains should reuse the lifecycle helpers instead of adding another horizontal review/export/archive chain.
next_priority: Run release gate for milestone closure, then move only to targeted trial defects, live-key diagnostics, or lifecycle primitive refactors that remove measured duplication.

residual_duplication_metrics.evidence: One helper definition plus artifact listing call sites across packet kinds.
One helper definition plus path-safe artifact retrieval call sites.
One helper definition plus metadata-only audit record call sites.
All four support packet kinds are represented in the zip profile table.

scope: P2.8 support and productized delivery horizontal lifecycle closeout

semantic_edges.evidence: Support packets build deterministic zip bundles through shared lifecycle contracts.
Verification records exclude raw keys, raw debug bundles, private payloads, and zip bytes.
Closure readiness is exposed through shared operational lifecycle projections.
Commercial report packets share readiness and lifecycle semantics with support packets.
Fast validation can detect drift in the P2.8 closeout maturity contract.
